GG_Sidedness

D. Grespin

2026-01-14

R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com.

When you click the Knit button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

Load Libraries

library(R.matlab)
## R.matlab v3.7.0 (2022-08-25 21:52:34 UTC) successfully loaded. See ?R.matlab for help.
## 
## Attaching package: 'R.matlab'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     getOption, isOpen
library(ggplot2)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2
library(ggpubr)
library(ggbreak)
## ggbreak v0.1.6 Learn more at https://yulab-smu.top/
## If you use ggbreak in published research, please cite the following
## paper:
## 
## S Xu, M Chen, T Feng, L Zhan, L Zhou, G Yu. Use ggbreak to effectively
## utilize plotting space to deal with large datasets and outliers.
## Frontiers in Genetics. 2021, 12:774846. doi: 10.3389/fgene.2021.774846
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(effectsize)

Load and arrange Matlab data

You can also embed plots, for example:

# read MATLAB .mat file into R (make sure to copy full path or place in your working directory)
distVintStruct1 <- readMat('/Users/drew.grespin/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UniversityofDenver/cav1_project/dataMats/confluentLine_dataMats/Paper/lineConfluentOut_forR_feb11.mat')
distVintStruct2 <- readMat('/Users/drew.grespin/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UniversityofDenver/cav1_project/dataMats/confluentLine_dataMats/Paper/lineConfluentOutRef_forR_feb11.mat')

# Load raw data
distVintRaw1     <- distVintStruct1$outDistVint[[1]]            # pull proper variable from MATLAB structure
dfRaw1 <- data.frame(
                     Normalized_Distance  = distVintRaw1[,1],
                     Normalized_Intensity = distVintRaw1[,2]
)

distVintRaw2     <- distVintStruct2$outDistVintRef[[1]]                   # pull proper variable from MATLAB structure
dfRaw2 <- data.frame(
                     Normalized_Distance  = distVintRaw2[,1],
                     Normalized_Intensity = distVintRaw2[,2]
)

Isolate desired data segments for analysis

You can also embed plots, for example:

# Split df1 into high and low

dfRaw1_rear  <- dfRaw1[dfRaw1$Normalized_Distance <= 0.5, ]
dfRaw1_front <- dfRaw1[dfRaw1$Normalized_Distance >  0.5, ]

# Split df2 into high and low
dfRaw2_rear   <- dfRaw2[dfRaw2$Normalized_Distance <= 0.5, ]
dfRaw2_front  <- dfRaw2[dfRaw2$Normalized_Distance >  0.5, ]

nrow(dfRaw1_rear)
## [1] 229287
nrow(dfRaw1_front)
## [1] 86966
nrow(dfRaw2_rear)
## [1] 229287
nrow(dfRaw2_front)
## [1] 86966

Set up data frame for plotting

dfPlot <- data.frame(
                     Intensity = c(
                       dfRaw1_rear$Normalized_Intensity,
                       dfRaw1_front$Normalized_Intensity,
                       dfRaw2_rear$Normalized_Intensity,
                       dfRaw2_front$Normalized_Intensity
                     ),
                     Protein = factor(c(
                       rep("Int",  length(dfRaw1_rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("Int",  length(dfRaw1_front$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("Ref",  length(dfRaw2_rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("Ref",  length(dfRaw2_front$Normalized_Intensity))
                     ), levels = c("Int", "Ref")),
                     
                     Region = factor(c(
                       rep("Rear",  length(dfRaw1_rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("Front", length(dfRaw1_front$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("Rear",  length(dfRaw2_rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("Front", length(dfRaw2_front$Normalized_Intensity))
                     ), levels = c("Rear", "Front"))
                    )

xpos_map <- c(
              "Int Rear"  = 1.0,
              "Int Front" = 1.4,
              "Ref Rear"  = 2.2,
              "Ref Front" = 2.6
             )

dfPlot$x_pos <- with(dfPlot,
                    ifelse(Protein == "Int",
                           ifelse(Region == "Rear", 1.0, 1.4),
                           ifelse(Region == "Rear", 1.9, 2.3)
                          )
                    )

Compute stats for custom box & whiskers (IQR)

box_stats <- dfPlot %>%
  group_by(Region, x_pos) %>%
  summarise(
            lower   = quantile(Intensity, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
            median  = quantile(Intensity, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),
            upper   = quantile(Intensity, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop"
           )

Compute stats for confidence interval & Cohen’s d

rear_int <- dfPlot %>%
            filter(Protein == "Int", Region == "Rear") %>%
            pull(Intensity)

front_int <- dfPlot %>%
             filter(Protein == "Int", Region == "Front") %>%
             pull(Intensity)

cohens_d(rear_int, front_int)
## Cohen's d |       95% CI
## ------------------------
## 0.16      | [0.15, 0.17]
## 
## - Estimated using pooled SD.
mean(rear_int)
## [1] 0.2726375
mean(front_int)
## [1] 0.2523855
mean(rear_int) - mean(front_int)
## [1] 0.020252
t.test(rear_int, front_int)$conf.int
## [1] 0.01935457 0.02114942
## attr(,"conf.level")
## [1] 0.95

Sample (for large datasets) and compute statistical significance between groups

You can also embed plots, for example:

# Rear comparison
pval_rear <- t.test(
                    dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "Int" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"],
                    dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "Ref" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"]
                   )$p.value

# Front comparison
pval_front <- t.test(
                     dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "Int" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"],
                     dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "Ref" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"]
                    )$p.value

pval_int <- t.test(
                   dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "Int" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"],
                   dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "Int" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"]
                  )$p.value

pval_ref <- t.test(
                   dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "Ref" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"],
                   dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "Ref" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"]
                  )$p.value

sig_lines <- data.frame(
  x_start = c(1.0, 1.4, 1.0, 1.9),       # x positions of Int groups
  x_end   = c(1.9, 2.3, 1.4, 2.3),       # x positions of Ref groups
  y       = c(1.25, 1.45, 1.05, 1.05),     # height above your plot
  label   = c(
    ifelse(pval_rear < 0.001, "***", ifelse(pval_rear < 0.01, "**", ifelse(pval_rear < 0.05, "*", "ns"))),
    ifelse(pval_front < 0.001, "***", ifelse(pval_front < 0.01, "**", ifelse(pval_front < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
  )
)

Preset theme elements

# Define colors
region_colors <- c("Rear" = "red", "Front" = "green")

# Define line parameters
median_length   <- 0.3
quartile_length <- 0.2

median_size   <- 4
quartile_size <- 3

# Define theme
plot_theme <- theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(size = 20, face = 'bold', hjust = 0.5, family = 'Arial'),
    axis.title         = element_text(size = 16, face = 'bold', hjust = 0.5, family = 'Arial'),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 48, hjust = 0.5, family = 'Arial', face = 'bold'),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 2, color = 'gray40'),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position    = "none"
  )

Setup plot features

boxplot <- ggplot(dfPlot, aes(x = x_pos, y = Intensity)) +
           # Raw points
           geom_jitter(
                       width = 0.14,
                       alpha = 0.05,
                       size  = 0.5,
                       color = "black"
           ) +
           # Vertical IQR line
           geom_segment(
                        data = box_stats,
                        aes(x = x_pos, xend = x_pos, y = lower, yend = upper, color = Region),
                        size = quartile_size
                       ) +
           # Q1 and Q3 horizontal lines
           geom_segment(
                        data = box_stats,
                        aes(
                            x     = x_pos - quartile_length / 2,
                            xend  = x_pos + quartile_length / 2,
                            y     = lower,
                            yend  = lower,
                            color = Region
                           ),
                           size = quartile_size
                       ) +
           geom_segment(
                        data = box_stats,
                        aes(
                            x     = x_pos - quartile_length / 2,
                            xend  = x_pos + quartile_length / 2,
                            y     = upper,
                            yend  = upper,
                            color = Region
                           ),
                           size = quartile_size
                       ) +
           # Median line
           geom_segment(
                        data = box_stats,
                        aes(
                            x     = x_pos - median_length / 2,
                            xend  = x_pos + median_length / 2,
                            y     = median,
                            yend  = median,
                            color = Region
                           ),
                        size = median_size
                       ) +
           #X/Y scales
           scale_x_continuous(
                              breaks = c(1.0, 1.4, 2.2, 2.6),
                              labels = c("", "", "", ""),
                              expand = c(0, 0)
                             ) +
           scale_y_continuous(
                              limits = c(0, 1.7),
                              breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25),
                              expand = c(0, 0)
                             ) +
           # Color of IQR lines
           scale_color_manual(
                              values = c(region_colors)  # combined for points and segments
                             ) +
           # Remove axis labels
           labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
           # add plot set up elements
           plot_theme
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

Add significance to plot

boxplot <- boxplot +
           geom_segment(
                        data = sig_lines,
                        aes(x = x_start,
                            xend = x_end,
                            y = y,
                            yend = y),
                        color = "black",
                        size = 4
                       ) +
           geom_text(
                       data = sig_lines,
                              aes(
                                  x = (x_start + x_end) / 2,
                                  y = y + ifelse(label == "ns", 0.17, 0.01),
                                  label = label
                                 ),
                              size = 30
                    )

Plot Output

boxplot
## Warning: Removed 164 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

ratio_df <- dfPlot %>%
  group_by(Protein, Region) %>%
  summarise(mean_int = mean(Intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Region, values_from = mean_int) %>%
  mutate(ratio = Rear / Front)
  
## Rear/Front ratio plot

ratio_plot <- ggplot(ratio_df, aes(x = Protein, y = ratio)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, fill = "grey40") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 32) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = ""
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x  = element_blank(),   # removes "5 10 15 20 10A"
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()   # removes tick marks
       ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
                              limits = c(0, 2.1),
                              breaks = seq(0, 2, 0.5),
                              expand = c(0, 0)
                             )
ratio_plot
